The main technological innovations that emerged include frequency regulation (known as ‘band-pass filtering’), multiple channels for voice or data communications, and signal strength indicators based on the distance between a transmitter and its receiving station. In addition, improvements such as duplex technology permitted for two independent signals to be communicated at different frequencies with only one power supply, reducing the effect of cochlear implant noise on received energy levels. Later on, multiplexing and code division spreaders could reduce the need for complex modulation schemes while allowing more bandwidth in data networks. Frequency modulation techniques also helped improve reception efficiency in the early 1900s, lowering average data rates before full duplex mode had been introduced later in the century. Finally, advancements in wireless receivers made it possible to receive transmissions over long distances without causing interference among other receivers, which also led to the development of digital systems for transmitting long audio/video sequences with less latency than through conventional media. By the mid 1930s, many countries began to adopt various forms of analog transmission, and new advances enabled both the United States Federal Government and several universities including MIT and Cambridge University Radio in the UK to develop commercial broadcast satellite technologies that enabled television, films and weather forecasts to be brought directly to audiences living hundreds of miles away. Digitalization of satellite operations followed relatively quickly in 1960 when the shortwave AM broadcast frequency bands were reduced to 10MHz and the International Telecommunications Union adopted frequency shift keying techniques so satellites would detect signals much better. This continued in the 1980s when satellite links became available throughout most continents except Antarctica. Currently, TV broadcasting of any kind can reach 99° north latitude latitudes across three regions covering some 70% of total land area – India, China and Africa. Global coverage now exceeds 50%. Angel Falls (Spanish: Salto Ángel; Pemon language: Kerepakupai Merú meaning "waterfall of the deepest place", or Parakupá Vená, meaning "the fall from the highest point") is a waterfall in Venezuela. It is the world's tallest uninterrupted waterfall, with a height of 979 metres (3,212 ft) and a plunge of 807 m (2,648 ft). The waterfall drops over the edge of the Auyán-tepui mountain in the Canaima National Park (Spanish: Parque Nacional Canaima), a UNESCO World Heritage site in the Gran Sabana region of Bolívar State. The height figure, 979 m (3,212 ft), mostly consists of the main plunge but also includes about 400 metres (1,300 ft) of sloped cascade and rapids below the drop and a 30-metre-high (100 ft) plunge downstream of the talus rapids.

History

Early-day growth

In October 1974, Jimmy Liautaud opened the original Jimmy John's restaurant on the corner of West Madison Street and North Franklin Avenue. It served only hot dogs (and later bratwurst). One year prior Liautaud had become frustrated with competing chains selling similar sandwiches at comparable prices when he saw the opportunity to expand. He set out to provide inexpensive lunchtime food for college students. When it grew "out of control," a friend offered him $1,000 if he could find two other customers willing to pay $60 [9]:
After looking around on campus Liautaud finally found customers who bought sandwiches for less than half the regular price. To keep it affordable Jimmy eventually began packaging each meal individually rather than serving it in large sandwiches under one roof. In doing so, Liautaud avoided the need to buy ingredients such as bread, mustard or pickles to add flavour to the meat patty[9]:36. He also cut down the number employees needed to make all three meals since one employee now usually handled all sides of the operation. Over a period of twenty years,[10]:20, 1,200 men between the ages of 14–30 formed the core base for Jimmy John's, along with the women employed for delivery etc., which helped lower cost. [7][10] The first unit that Liautaud obtained was located at 38 N. Michigan Ave; his wife Barbara took six months before she could get anyone else behind the wall to purchase their goods (due to her name appearing on credit applications), thus starting from 0 sales after hiring only one person, but not paying rent [8]. Liautaud soon expanded into apartment buildings where they leased units to residents who worked nearby. However their expansion in apartments slowed in 1979 mainly caused by rising retail rents for Chicago suburbs which were far greater that Jimmy John's' business expense. Since then management decided to continue expanding primarily through direct marketting.[13][7]:22-23 It did this with a focus on university dining halls across the country because students tended to travel

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
What are the key benefits of Delta Live Tables?

### Response:
generated_text:
A table shows data about two or more items in one field when all fields of the record have values with the same value (that is, "yes"). Example: "Yes" for customer and billing information (the two terms are actually different, but in practice you probably don't care!) A delta table uses the unique ID to identify which tables were updated while other tables remained unchanged during this interval. The unique ID identifies every time your object interacts with an external system such as a client database - for example, by passing it its own internal reference in the query string, if needed. That way each row can be accessed independently. Most important, with a single delta table, your applications stay independent; it's not necessary that they work together!

If multiple fields of your records are referenced from within a record using only field values, this is not really considered a delta table because most programs would already use full-text search (or text mining). However,Delta Live Tables allow dynamic integration between systems running on very little memory, making it possible to easily store large amounts of data without being limited to disk space. Each entry contains a delta table reference with the corresponding field values. With Delta Live Tables, accessing the records stored inside a delta table doesn't require access to any specific resource and the program can continue processing the data and outputting results (with the exception of reading data out of file for processing into other files) even though files get swapped around dynamically (i.e., their contents change over time in network sessions). That makes them ideal for handling batch data like payroll information or sales reports. You could also use these kinds of databases to display messages instead of rows within documents. Or if used by an application to process business rules, your program couldn't access the records unless they had the specified characteristics associated with them - for example a field has certain letter codes, and so forth. Of course, you'd need to test the application before doing anything with it...and make sure everything worked correctly, because it takes up almost no extra storage space. But in many cases it won't cost significantly more than any simple DBMS, although there might be slightly less flexibility available since it requires fewer features for managing large datasets (so few functions will ever operate simultaneously!).

generated_text:
A zipping machine divides zippers into groups called segments and each segment must be opened manually to obtain materials. Segments are numbered starting 0 for one end, 1 for another end and 2 etc., where “1” is all open in front and “2” all closed behind. Each new segment gets its own zipper to join it or split a previous segment when needed. A zipper cannot close normally without removing the top of another segment; but if two halves don't come together, the zipper turns inside out because of gravity and can no longer move material through the opening. Finally, a zipper can also go vertical, like a belt-drive sewing machine. So long as the Zipper Segment (0 - 2) remains upright, any other Zippers get their pieces taken from adjacent machines by moving the last Zipper back up into place while keeping a tight eye on any other Zippers being moved around so they do not get separated. After the Russo-Japanese War, the First Treaty was signed on 30 July 1907 by Motono Ichirō, the Japanese Ambassador in Moscow, and Alexander Izvolsky, the Foreign Minister of Russian. The treaty was divided into two parts: one is open agreement, which respected the treaties concluded between the two countries and China, respected China's independence, promoted open doors, and achieved equal opportunities and another is secret agreement, which defined the scope of Japan's interests in Southern Manchuria and Russia's interests in Northern Manchuria and Japan recognized Russia's interests in Outer Mongolia, and Russia recognized Japan's interests in the Korean Peninsula.

In January 1900, a delegation of high officials from China accompanied Izwolsky at his trip to Germany; all those people who went were invited back but not given official status as members of the Chinese Cabinet as they would later be called. So no Chinese foreign minister visited Russia ever again, while in 1905 Tsar Nicholas II started his three year stay in America (1905–07) without being an ambassador.

During his long absence in Europe he was only represented by Russian representatives and a special envoy for Asia affairs. From 1903 the Consulate General was set up under the direction of Major General Makhno in New York City. Although a formal embassy existed from 1906 with the arrival of the first consul general to Tashkent (now Tashkent), the consulate served mainly as the central administrative body and therefore, a representative presence abroad even if it included staffs assigned to specific areas. It also acted primarily as intermediary through negotiations between government departments or when diplomatic incidents led to direct contacts among different actors, including between Japan and other foreign powers.[4] After the 1904 Russo-Turkish War, however, both parties realized that Japan’s military base had been undermined during this conflict so Tsar Nicholas agreed to construct its successor in Korea instead—the Kumano Base. From then on Russia took over the administration of Korea from Japan. During his visit to the United States from June 18 -20 1925 Tsar Nicholas II gave lectures to students sponsored by the American Chamber Institute. The next important step towards establishing close ties between the Soviet Union and Japan occurred in 1922 when the new Japanese ambassador met the Secretary-General’s representative Tsuneji Masao Goto at Shimonoseki Palace. At that point, the Japanese Foreign Ministry established what became known as the Japanese Secretariat of the Organization Coordination Group. This post covered relations within organizations involving Japan, the Central Asian Countries, and Pacific Islands. The office continued until 1939[5]. Until 1944 the Secretariat shared jurisdiction over relations with South China Sea Areas and Southeast East Asia.

### Instruction:
What is gardening?

### Input:
Gardening is the practice of growing and cultivating plants as part of horticulture. In gardens, ornamental plants are often grown for their flowers, foliage, or overall appearance; useful plants, such as root vegetables, leaf vegetables, fruits, and herbs, are grown for consumption, for use as dyes, or for medicinal or cosmetic use.

Gardening ranges in scale from fruit orchards, to long boulevard plantings with one or more different types of shrubs, trees, and herbaceous plants, to residential back gardens including lawns and foundation plantings, all the way to container gardens grown inside or outside. Gardening may be very specialized, with only one type of plant grown, or involve a variety of plants in mixed plantings. It involves an active participation in the growing of plants, and tends to be labor-intensive, which differentiates it from farming or forestry.

Ask your child to wear protective clothing or other gear when playing outside in grass, dust, dirt, etc., if the kids will be able to do so safely and comfortably. You can limit outdoor activities for children who have allergies only as long as their reactions are mild enough such that they won't feel sick from too much exposure (eg. sneezing). But many allergens irritate an infant's eyes and nose, making it difficult for you to ask her to cover up completely while on a hike. The best solution would be to keep them at home temporarily rather than risk being bitten by rattlesnakes or bee stings because the itching and/or soreness caused may not go away overnight. In addition, there are certain foods -- even milk -- which cause allergic symptoms without any obvious link between allergens and food allergies. If your child eats any of those foods (for example, almond meal cereal), consider restricting his diet slightly until he figures out what triggers his allergies (i.e., eliminating grains from a child that cannot tolerate peanuts).

The American College of Allergy, Asthma & Immunology recommends one daily trip outdoors for every 2-2½ years to prevent allergies but this varies depending upon where the patient lives. For more information about traveling with your child please see our Guidelines for Travel With Your Child's Medical Specialists page. The AAP suggests taking precautions before leaving for travel with asthma or allergies (see below) if allergies are serious, but less so for most conditions including childhood eye ailments such as allergies to pollen or dust mites!

**Please note: some doctors might say we need to restrict outdoor activity for children with allergies since we don't know where the allergies stem from! What is Assassin's Creed?

### Input:
Assassin's Creed is an open-world, action-adventure, and stealth game franchise published by Ubisoft and developed mainly by its studio Ubisoft Montreal using the game engine Anvil and its more advanced derivatives. Created by Patrice Désilets, Jade Raymond, and Corey May, the Assassin's Creed video game series depicts a fictional millennia-old struggle between the Order of Assassins, who fight for peace and free will, and the Knights Templar, who desire peace through order and control. The series features historical fiction, science fiction, and fictional characters intertwined with real-world historical events and historical figures. In most games, players control a historical Assassin while also playing as an Assassin Initiate or someone caught in the Assassin–Templar conflict in the present-day framing story. Considered a spiritual successor to the Prince of Persia series, Assassin's Creed took inspiration from the novel Alamut by the Slovenian writer Vladimir Bartol, based on the historical Hashashin sect of the medieval Middle East.

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task, paired with an input that provides further context. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
What are some languages that feature hygienic macros?

### Input:
Hygienic macros are macros whose expansion is guaranteed not to cause the accidental capture of identifiers. They are a feature of programming languages such as Scheme,[1] Dylan,[2] Rust, Nim, and Julia. The general problem of accidental capture was well known within the Lisp community prior to the introduction of hygienic macros. Macro writers would use language features that would generate unique identifiers (e.g., gensym) or use obfuscated identifiers in order to avoid the problem. Hygienic macros are a programmatic solution to the capture problem that is integrated into the macro expander itself. The term "hygiene" was coined in Kohlbecker et al.'s 1986 paper that introduced hygienic macro expansion, inspired by the terminology used in mathematics.[3]

Additionally, the day also signifies the triumph of good over evil, as it commemorates the victory of Vishnu as Narasimha Narayana over Hiranyakashipu.Holi is originated and is predominantly celebrated in the Indian subcontinent but has also spread to other regions of Asia and parts of the Western world through the Indian diaspora.

Holi also celebrates the arrival of Spring in India, the end of winter, and the blossoming of love. It is also an invocation for a good spring harvest season. It lasts for a night and a day, starting on the evening of the Purnima (Full Moon Day) falling in the Hindu calendar month of Phalguna, which falls around the middle of March in the Gregorian calendar.

Who was the software chief during the first release of Apple Siri?

When I was an employee there really wasn't anyone who handled Siri. The only person with responsibility involved was the engineer tasked with writing the code behind it as part of the iOS team working under me (Phil Schiller). This same engineer worked from his home office; since my cofounder Mike Cook had a job outside the company at Google it seemed like a bad idea from an ethical perspective to put him directly in front of a computer screen, so he would use other people's computers rather than having someone show up physically at our offices. Once he came into our building early for his team meeting one evening and introduced himself and talked briefly through what he'd been doing for 12 hours straight — how long did you think we should tell employees they could work away from normal business hours without running afoul of our rules, given their privacy concerns? We didn’t have answers yet but by that point we realized there must be some middle ground between letting remote workers telecommute indefinitely and having them always on site because it wasn’t clear who might need help or needed to know immediately if something went wrong with their commute back later. ### Instruction:
Name a style of hot yoga.

### Response:
generated_text:
Hot Yoga

#1

In one sense, your body and mind feel like they are not being cared for, even if you think so yourself or others do not perceive this behavior. But most likely it’s because some people just can’t give everything 100 percent effort to take care of others around them who may need more attention than they might be able to offer at any given time in their life and on the days when things don’t go (unexpectedly) exactly as planned. That means a part of us feels neglected, whether we realize it consciously out loud or subconsciously from behind our own behaviors, which we call “selfishness”. The reason why the other person does seem distant sometimes is probably the result of stress (that goes both ways). When there isn't enough peace within ourselves for the other person to get close to us properly - this distance gets manifested unconsciously into a less-close relationship. It seems silly to think we shouldn’t worry about doing that since what we tend to notice first is physical touch - yes! Sometimes I wonder, how much have I really been contributing towards my partner? If not that much, then maybe…maybe it's too little and he/she could benefit greatly by looking a bit hard, especially in public places where the world is made less open air...at least, that would make things interesting and challenging but still wouldn’t kill anyone 😀

I am working daily with my students through conscious practice techniques which help me understand these situations as well. As a consequence I'm feeling closer and connected with myself personally and also with other people, i.e. friends, families et al. As another possible approach - perhaps it is necessary that we stop giving 100% effort, and also start recognizing the fact that people have different needs, which should not require that we always strive towards those goals regardless of who they are or their needs may look like. We should live within space limitations and boundaries without getting overly focused only upon our own issues, otherwise something might slip under the radar and become ignored instead of getting resolved quickly and efficiently. Even though we've all got stuff running here and there; it is up to each one of us to keep doing it right after finishing the work of course. Also important factor: Don't try to impress everyone else. A lot of these problems can be avoided altogether if we pay more attention to our own feelings rather thinking to the consequences...then our actions will naturally follow automatically.

In business terminology, fixed assets have been deemed as either cash or inventory. It can be difficult to compare whether it is really “fixed” or not since, over time it still exists in some form and requires maintenance regardless of whatever changes may take place on a day-to-day basis. For example: If you have all your invoices from May and you expect to generate another invoice for July and so forth through June; there will always have to be new invoices generated at other times since they will never have gone completely out-of-balance (i.e., paid). The term used by many finance professionals is that if one gets paid three days later than their due date, then your debt is no longer current but it becomes a deferred liability rather than currently owing money because the invoice has already gone into arrears – which would otherwise become current immediately upon payment ($0). Some banks call this negative gearing which means they do take tax deductions as well as avoiding their own capital requirements – even though their cash reserves are not low enough! So you could ask, does borrowing interest free/discounted payments help ease overall bank profits when interest rates change annually? To answer those questions we need to determine how profitable borrowing interest is for companies such as ourselves where most of our revenue comes in the form of contracts. Note that you don't borrow $1000 each month for 10 years...rather you repay 6 months (2 x $500) @ 8% during September and 3 months (for 4 x $250) at the end of February with only minimum principal payments required thereafter until December 31st in 2013 - or the entire life of the contract and beyond - whichever it happens to last ;) There certainly exists situations, however, of financial distress when companies fail as creditors lose a lot of monies. We also face this same dilemma since it might cost us more to pay our bills monthly than its worth, especially considering the risks involved both ways – as in whether taking additional equity now for debt repayment will allow them to recover quickly if things go south before you receive that check to your email - and thereby save yourself quite a bit of money compared to what was originally borrowed. In any case its important to understand that if you can afford to give up some interest rate savings/profit by being taken off debt in advance instead of after losing everything – it's definitely smart investment decision…not just prudent investment!!!

The table below lists the standard Gibbs function of formation for several elements and chemical compounds and is taken from Lange's Handbook of Chemistry. Note that all values are in kJ/mol. Far more extensive tables can be found in the CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics and the NIST JANAF tables.[1] The NIST Chemistry WebBook (see link below) is an online resource that contains standard enthalpy of formation for various compounds along with the standard absolute entropy for these compounds from which the standard Gibbs free energy of formation can be calculated.

However, reactions such as oxidation are not as direct – some of the CO molecules may remain in a gas phase after oxidation, generating other reactions (such as reduction) that could add to the resulting value. It’s imperative that when training you don't forget the importance of this shot, even if you aren’t playing on tour. There is zero risk associated with executing this volley; however I suggest you spend about five minutes trying multiple options before committing yourself to any particular sequence of directions until you feel confident enough to go ahead and use it from match point on out! In terms of execution, just find different angles to approach the ball, be patient with your release on your wrist roll, etc… Before hitting this shot for the first time I strongly recommend holding the racket head slightly above the palm side surface of the racquet handle allowing plenty room to move it around and then allow the racquet head to drop a bit before putting too much weight behind it leaving quite a few inches on the ball. Most importantly, remember not to jump in early...wait till the last second before throwing downward bodyweight into it!!! The Malibamat'so forms the northern arm of the Katse Dam reservoir, a part of the Lesotho Highlands Water Project. ### Response:
generated_text:
Germany uses a proportional voter-transferrable single transferable vote.The electorate consists of citizens aged 18 or over, who are entitled to participate in elections but no more than 4 of whom would be able to cast votes at one election. The other 3 are only entitled to vote for local officials and parties directly elected by municipal districts. All votes must go either for or against a candidate on the ballot paper whose name appears opposite his/her constituency’s representative symbol located on top left (for electoral district elections) or right (for legislative body elections). For every person entitled to vote there is no limit. On occasion candidates lose their party label; however most voters can keep casting ballots regardless unless the list is cut off before Election Day - then they need not vote. If two different lists represent opposing views about which position should be taken – each may choose 2 from that choice; no decision can be made until both complete those selections. No individual is permitted to present a nomination paper without it being subject to scrutiny and being accepted as read by others so far down in line its title. Each regional council can have members serve two three year terms consecutively within each other region. They cannot hold office simultaneously for two consecutive three year periods otherwise they automatically resign for the ‘shortening term’. After completing seven of nine years any Regional Council member will become eligible to run again.
Any registered voter is allowed to register as many times per year as the Council dictates and as many different address within Germany. Voters do require identification papers that include biographical information (e.g., proof of identity), national ID number(s) and valid residence card when registering first time on local ballots in Germany after having lived abroad. Once properly verified voters may change addresses in areas with less restrictive voting rules during local elections held between September 1st through May 14th of the following year. Registration cards provide all voter entitlements including registration date, polling place, polling station name(s), ward and division numbers, official election designation code (a numerical value assigned to certain political campaigns by the Federal Interior Ministry), seat location or precinct details if requested specifically indicating “vote center (DFL)” instead of your usual neighborhood poll station name and last four digits of Social Insurance Number that you use personally.
Election day polling booths generally open 30 minutes after polling centers close 10 minutes after, depending upon town. Polling stations are opened approximately 15 to 20 seconds apart. Voters should note the approximate time given for returning ballots. Ballot boxes often have seals

To start off the pie making process place ingredients into a 9 × 13 inch baking dish and set aside at least 30 minutes after placing them. Remove from the oven and lay the pan directly onto cool cooling rack and set aside until cold. Transfer pecans to a serving plate and brush on any remaining glaze which will also help keep the nuts warm. Serve alongside ice cream or mixed fruit pudding. A line of the equation y = x, representing perfect model performance, is sometimes added as a reference. Where the model successfully reproduces a benchmark, that point will lie on the line.

Parity plots are found in scientific papers and reports, when the author wishes to validate a model in a visual way. However, when the data have a wide range, the large scale makes important discrepancies invisible and the model appears better than it actually is. In that case, a plot of model errors [(y-x) vs. x] is better for evaluating the performance of the model.

### Input:
In economics, inflation is an increase in the general price level of goods and services in an economy.[3][4][5][6] When the general price level rises, each unit of currency buys fewer goods and services; consequently, inflation corresponds to a reduction in the purchasing power of money.[7][8] The opposite of inflation is deflation, a decrease in the general price level of goods and services. The common measure of inflation is the inflation rate, the annualized percentage change in a general price index.[9] As prices faced by households do not all increase at the same rate, the consumer price index (CPI) is often used for this purpose. The employment cost index is also used for wages in the United States.

### Instruction:
Given the following paragraph on cell structure, what is an example of membrane proteins?

### Input:
Every cell is enclosed within a cell membrane that separates its cytoplasm from the extracellular space.[41] A cell membrane consists of a lipid bilayer, including cholesterols that sit between phospholipids to maintain their fluidity at various temperatures. Cell membranes are semipermeable, allowing small molecules such as oxygen, carbon dioxide, and water to pass through while restricting the movement of larger molecules and charged particles such as ions.[42] Cell membranes also contains membrane proteins, including integral membrane proteins that go across the membrane serving as membrane transporters, and peripheral proteins that loosely attach to the outer side of the cell membrane, acting as enzymes shaping the cell.[43] Cell membranes are involved in various cellular processes such as cell adhesion, storing electrical energy, and cell signalling and serve as the attachment surface for several extracellular structures such as a cell wall, glycocalyx, and cytoskeleton.

### Instruction:
Which is a species of fish? Blowfish or Toucan

### Response:
generated_text:
Toucan-type fish with small wings and long tail, which can be very agile. They are usually fast and they will run away if challenged by predators (eagles). These fast creatures swim in a line as they dive to seek prey underwater for their sharp teeth. The adults cannot be confused with other birds because they have large, orange beaks (which look like "beak bones") at each end of the head. Unlike eagles, toucans have no feathers on their heads, so this makes them hard to see under water in case predator is following it. Some even fly upside down! In addition, when attacked by sharks in some part of the world, toucans go under the surface until it passes, so they do not really need protection from being eaten. On the whole, toucans appear fearless. They avoid boats because most boats are often used to transport food, particularly shark. This may discourage fishing since there must always a boat nearby that takes care of those dangerous animals, but without it, toucans die after eating little fish instead of trying to escape. Most importantly, toucans spend all day sitting up front of waterfowl colonies watching what birds try to catch using fishing lines, nets and hooks (see illustration). If they spot something approaching, they tend not to fly out of the way until it stops near enough to swallow the animal whole and then begins digesting its meal. When you encounter a toucan you might think about feeding it! It does not want your bait; if you offer it, give it one piece right away and leave and never return unless it swallows. Avoid giving more than two pieces.

It is regulated by the unofficial Norwegian Academy, which translates the name as 'Standard Norwegian'. The other is Høgnorsk ('High Norwegian'), a more purist form of Nynorsk, which maintains the language in an original form as given by Ivar Aasen and rejects most of the reforms from the 20th century; this form has limited use.

Voorhees Chapel subsequently hosted annual festivals celebrating both women’s education and Jewish culture, including Purim celebrations and Menorah liturgies. More recently, the chapel has been used as administrative offices. It appears it houses some of the original artifacts associated with the original women’s programs—a display case containing items like Voorhees gowns and a book titled “The Story of Our Life.” The plaque inscribed next to her name inside was given to the chapel through a large anonymous donors, and refers to her contributions to the school beginning in 1923 when she created a "Pursuit Scholarship Fund" for $1 per week which offered funding for tuition assistance to women graduating seniors applying for higher education or vocational training elsewhere. In 1978 a committee presented Voorhees a scholarship to graduate student Margaret Mudd Ehrlich called this ‘Margaret Ruth Schultz Award for Contribution’, honoring the memory of a long time friend who donated a fund to support graduate students at Rutgers known today as the Margaret Ruth Schultz Graduate Scholarship. The current scholarship provides full-tuition reimbursement for students entering their second or third year with an undergraduate degree, while providing 100% financial aid awards to undergrads pursuing a master’s program and above. Students should contact the Office of Student Affairs/Student Diversity Services at ruralsuburban@rutgers.edu if they are interested in becoming a recipient.

### Input:
Gandhi's first major achievement came in 1917 with the Champaran agitation in Bihar. The Champaran agitation pitted the local peasantry against largely Anglo-Indian plantation owners who were backed by the local administration. The peasants were forced to grow indigo (Indigofera sp.), a cash crop for Indigo dye whose demand had been declining over two decades, and were forced to sell their crops to the planters at a fixed price. Unhappy with this, the peasantry appealed to Gandhi at his ashram in Ahmedabad. Pursuing a strategy of nonviolent protest, Gandhi took the administration by surprise and won concessions from the authorities.

or divisions that share common history and culture, as well as specific linguistic groups.
The Alaskans can be lumped into two general categories-Beringians and Yupik (Kuskokwim/Dakota). A third major group exists with members from other cultures. There also have been claims made about a few smaller tribes before 1700 including Dena'ina, Chilkat (Southeast Coast), and Unalaska (Western North America). I put on my shoes and socks so they wouldn’t get dirty while I went for a hike. And then I realized… That meant wearing them too long would make them look awful! Not good, right?!?! Yes, but after all, who wears old ones?? Anyway, at least now my nice new pair won’t get worn out, yay!! So, I kept that trick up with every other pair until one day… I took off both shoes and socks simultaneously without taking off either sock first 🙂
(My husband didn’t approve.)

When writing adverbial sentences where different words occur together, such as the word “will” above, include the word “are”; otherwise your response sounds unclear.
For example:
She has a good point! We need to get her in tomorrow!
Will she go? Maybe yes, maybe no? In which case we better think of some excuse :| She seems quite energetic today …

What is Sinking Sand?

### Input:
Quicksand, also known as sinking sand, is a colloid consisting of fine granular material (such as sand, silt or clay) and water. It forms in saturated loose sand when the sand is suddenly agitated. When water in the sand cannot escape, it creates a liquefied soil that loses strength and cannot support weight. Quicksand can form in standing water or in upward flowing water (as from an artesian spring). In the case of upward flowing water, forces oppose the force of gravity and suspend the soil particles.

Most cells grow by a process called budding/planting which begins with one round (called a zygote) containing just 1 cell as a seedling. As the germ layer comes into contact
with the developing tissue, new tissues develop continuously until the zygotes take off. Once inside the egg yolk and developing nuclei (in adults!) cells start to be organized more like seeds (aka fruits.) It's also possible that fruit will develop from some form
of budding although we don't have evidence of that yet! We do know that the plant-guttate line was present since earliest common descent but doesn't really get explored much because it was too difficult to study; that wasn't something studied

wasn't until later on that the first species formed with lots of generations.
